Name: Pansage
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

Name: Simisage
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

Name: Sewaddle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

Name: Sewaddle
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.

Name: Swadloon
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

Name: Leavanny
Effects: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

Name: Leavanny
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They go wherever the wind takes them. On rainy days, their bodies are heavier, so they take shelter beneath big trees.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

Name: Whimsicott
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon can only use that attack.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Riding whirlwinds, they appear. These Pokémon sneak through gaps into houses and cause all sorts of mischief.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

Name: Deerling
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons.

Name: Sawsbuck
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The plants growing on its horns change according to the season. The leaders of the herd possess magnificent horns.

Name: Virizion
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Sacred Sword during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

Name: Pansear
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600º F.

Name: Simisear
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A flame burns inside its body. It scatters embers from its head and tail to sear its opponents.

Name: Darumaka
Flavor-Text: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its internal fire burns at 2,500º F, making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.

Name: Panpour
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It does not thrive in dry environments. It keeps itself damp by shooting water stored in its head tuft from its tail.

Name: Simipour
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [G] Energy attached to it, heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Basculin
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this Pokémon does 80 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

Name: Basculin
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Red and blue Basculin usually do not get along, but sometimes members of one school mingle with the other’s school.

Name: Ducklett
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon can’t retreat during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

Name: Swanna
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It administers sharp, powerful pecks with its bill. It whips its long neck to deliver forceful repeated strikes.

Name: Cubchoo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

Name: Cubchoo
Flavor-Text: Its nose is always running. It sniffs the snot back up because the mucus provides the raw material for its moves.

Name: Beartic
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

Name: Beartic
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It freezes its breath to create fangs and claws of ice to fight with. Cold northern areas are its habitat.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its membranes and released while it is gliding.

Name: Joltik
Flavor-Text: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

Name: Thundurus
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

Name: Woobat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

Name: Swoobat
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.

Name: Venipede
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive.

Name: Whirlipede
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It is usually motionless, but when attacked, it rotates at high speed and then crashes into its opponent.

Name: Scolipede
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

Name: Gothita
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

Name: Gothita
Flavor-Text: They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, they are looking at something that only Gothita can see.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

Name: Gothitelle
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Gothitelle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They can predict the future from the placement and movement of the stars. They can see Trainers’ life spans.

Name: Roggenrola
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 40 or less.
Flavor-Text: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

Name: Roggenrola
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.

Name: Boldore
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has [F] Resistance, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

Name: Boldore
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.

Name: Gigalith
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are [F] Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
Does 20 more damage for each [F] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The solar energy absorbed by its body’s orange crystals is magnified internally and fired from its mouth.

Name: Drilbur
Effects: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon’s attacks does 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

Name: Drilbur
Flavor-Text: It makes its way swiftly through the soil by putting both claws together and rotating at high speed.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

Name: Throh
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

Name: Sawk
Effects: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Tying their belts gets them pumped and makes their punches more destructive. Disturbing their training angers them.

Name: Sandile
Flavor-Text: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

Name: Krokorok
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

Name: Krookodile
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

Name: Terrakion
Effects: This Pokémon can’t use Sacred Sword during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can’t help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Vullaby
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: They tend to guard their posteriors with suitable bones they have found. They pursue weak Pokémon.

Name: Mandibuzz
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Does 10 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Watching from the sky, they swoop to strike weakened Pokémon on the ground. They decorate themselves with bones.

Name: Ferroseed
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They stick their spikes into cave walls and absorb the minerals they find in the rock.

Name: Ferroseed
Flavor-Text: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They attach themselves to cave ceilings, firing steel spikes at targets passing beneath them.

Name: Ferrothorn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

Name: Klink
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Interlocking two bodies and spinning around generates the energy they need to live.

Name: Klang
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Spinning minigears are rotated at high speed and repeatedly fired away. It is dangerous if the gears don’t return.

Name: Klinklang
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t use Zap Cannon during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The gear with the red core is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Sacred Sword during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

Name: Patrat
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Extremely cautious, they take shifts to maintain a constant watch of their nest. They feel insecure without a lookout.

Name: Watchog
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent’s deck and put them back on top of his or her deck in any order.
Before doing damage, you may flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

Name: Pidove
Flavor-Text: Each follows its Trainer’s orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

Name: Tranquill
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Many people believe that, deep in the forest where Tranquill live, there is a peaceful place where there is no war.

Name: Unfezant
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Males have plumage on their heads. They will never let themselves fell close to anyone other than their Trainers.

Name: Audino
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Heal 50 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its auditory sense is astounding. It has a radar-like ability to understand its surrounding through slight sounds.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Their white fur feels amazing to touch. Their fur repels dust and prevents static electricity from building up.

Name: Rufflet
Flavor-Text: They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.

Name: Rufflet
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

Name: Braviary
Effects: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Bianca
Description: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Cheren
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Crushing Hammer
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Great Ball
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Max Potion
Description: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Catcher
Description: Switch your opponent’s Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Recycle
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Thundurus
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.
